title: Bailey Console Table with Drawers
summary: A two-drawer console with plywood side panels on 2x2 legs, topped with a breadboard-end plank top. (Source titles it 'with Doors,' but the plan builds drawers.)
description:
A tidy console table with two drawers, plywood side panels, and a plank top with breadboard ends, on square 2x2 legs. Worth noting up front: the source calls this 'Bailey Console Table with Doors,' but the actual cut list, hardware, and steps all build drawers on euro slides — no door panels or hinges anywhere. This plan follows the buildable content, which is drawers.

The frame is a pocket-hole exercise with one rule to remember: use 3/4" pocket-hole settings whenever any 1x material is in the joint, and 1-1/2" settings only when both pieces are 2x. The plywood side panels tuck between the legs, pocket holes hidden on the inside, and the whole frame must be checked for square before the back cross-braces it.

The drawers are the intermediate part. Build them to the openings and the 14" euro slides, install them inset 3/4" so the applied 1x12 faces land flush, and adjust until they glide. Attach the faces last with a 1/8" gap all around, screwed from inside. The top is a simple 1x6 glue-up with breadboard ends, attached from below after you pull the drawers out to get at the frame.

steps.title: Build the frame
steps.body: Build the console frame with pocket holes. Use 3/4" pocket-hole settings whenever ANY 1x material is in the joint, and 1-1/2" settings only when joining 2x to 2x. Getting the setting right for each joint keeps the screws from blowing through thin stock.

steps.title: Attach the side panels
steps.body: Drill 3/4" pocket holes along the side and top edges of the plywood panels and attach them to the legs, pocket holes on the inside, flush to the inside of the project. Check the whole frame for square — the back will lock it, but only if it's square first.

steps.title: Attach the bottom aprons
steps.body: Attach the bottom aprons/stretchers with 3/4" pocket holes, hiding them on the back and inside faces.

steps.title: Attach the back
steps.body: Attach the 1/4" back with nails and glue to square and stiffen the cabinet.

steps.title: Build and install the drawers
steps.body: Build the two drawer boxes from 1x8 with 1/4" bottoms, then install them on the 14" slides inset 3/4" from the front of the cabinet. Adjust until the drawers slide easily.

steps.title: Attach the drawer faces
steps.body: Attach the 1x12 drawer faces to the boxes, leaving a 1/8" gap around all sides. Secure from inside the box with 1-1/4" screws, and mount the knobs.

steps.title: Build and attach the top
steps.body: Build the top with 3/4" pocket holes and add the breadboard ends. Remove the drawers, then attach the top to the frame with 1-1/4" screws and glue. Sand and finish.

images.alt: Console table with two drawers, plywood side panels, and a breadboard-end top
